jCarouselLite – Demo

Interactive carousels showing various configuration options.

Default Configuration

The carousel is created with the "next" and "prev" navigation buttons. You don't have to specify anything else unless you want to bend it to your will.

‹
› 
$(".default .carousel").jCarouselLite({
    btnNext: ".default .next",
    btnPrev: ".default .prev"
});

Auto-Scroll NEW

Automatically scrolls content based on the time period given in the auto option.

$(".auto .carousel").jCarouselLite({
    auto: 800,
    speed: 1000
});

Scroll More Items NEW

You can scroll more than one item at a time using the scroll option. Provide the number of items to scroll per button click.

‹
› 
$(".scrollMore .carousel").jCarouselLite({
    btnNext: ".scrollMore .next",
    btnPrev: ".scrollMore .prev",
    scroll: 2
});

Mouse Wheel Navigation NEW

Use the mouse wheel to scroll the carousel. This is not mutually exclusive with navigation buttons — you can use both. Requires the Mouse Wheel plugin.

$(".mouseWheel .carousel").jCarouselLite({
    mouseWheel: true
});

Mouse Wheel & Navigation Buttons NEW

Use both mouse wheel and navigation buttons on the same carousel. Requires the Mouse Wheel plugin.

‹
› 
$(".mouseWheelButtons .carousel").jCarouselLite({
    btnNext: ".mouseWheelButtons .next",
    btnPrev: ".mouseWheelButtons .prev",
    mouseWheel: true
});

Custom Animation – Easing Effects

Supply any jQuery easing name to get custom animation curves. This example uses easeOutBounce. Requires the Easing plugin.

‹
› 
$(".bounceout .carousel").jCarouselLite({
    btnNext: ".bounceout .next",
    btnPrev: ".bounceout .prev",
    easing: "easeOutBounce",
    speed: 1000
});

Custom Animation – Speed Control

Fast or slow — specify an animation speed in milliseconds.

‹
› 
$(".slower .carousel").jCarouselLite({
    btnNext: ".slower .next",
    btnPrev: ".slower .prev",
    speed: 800
});

More or Less

You can increase or decrease the number of visible items using the visible option.

‹
› 
$(".moreItems .carousel").jCarouselLite({
    btnNext: ".moreItems .next",
    btnPrev: ".moreItems .prev",
    visible: 2
});

Non-Image Content

You are not limited to images. Any HTML content works inside carousel items.

‹
  • Item 1: Plain Text
  • Item 2: Bold Text
  • Item 3: A link
  • Item 4: List Item
› 
$(".nonImageContent .carousel").jCarouselLite({
    btnNext: ".nonImageContent .next",
    btnPrev: ".nonImageContent .prev"
});

Vertical

Set vertical: true to make the carousel scroll vertically instead of horizontally.

‹
› 
$(".vertical .carousel").jCarouselLite({
    btnNext: ".vertical .next",
    btnPrev: ".vertical .prev",
    vertical: true
});

Image Slider

A full-width image slider — set visible: 1 to show one large image at a time.

‹
› 
$(".imageSlider .carousel").jCarouselLite({
    btnNext: ".imageSlider .next",
    btnPrev: ".imageSlider .prev",
    visible: 1,
    speed: 500
});

Fraction Configuration

Pass a decimal to visible (e.g. 2.5) to tease the next item — a helpful UX hint that the carousel continues.

‹
› 
$(".fraction .carousel").jCarouselLite({
    btnNext: ".fraction .next",
    btnPrev: ".fraction .prev",
    visible: 2.5,
    circular: false
});

Non Circular

Carousels are circular by default. Set circular: false to stop at the ends instead of wrapping around.

‹
› 
$(".nonCircular .carousel").jCarouselLite({
    btnNext: ".nonCircular .next",
    btnPrev: ".nonCircular .prev",
    circular: false
});
